fighter.origin: Trained in the militia halls of Harmonia, Fighters learn that strength alone wins nothing -- discipline does.
fighter.worldview: The world is a battlefield. Every problem has a direct solution if you hit it hard enough.
flavor_quotes: My sword arm is my argument.
I don't retreat. I advance in a different direction.
The best defense is an overwhelming offense.

cleric.origin: Clerics study in the Ivory Sanctum, learning that healing is not kindness -- it is strategy. A living ally deals more damage than a dead one.
cleric.worldview: Everyone deserves healing. Not everyone deserves it first.
flavor_quotes: I heal because the alternative is paperwork.
The light guides me. Mostly toward people making poor decisions.
Raise is not a reward. It is a second chance you should not need.

mage.origin: Mages emerge from the Obsidian Academy, where knowledge is the only currency and fire is the preferred negotiation tactic.
mage.worldview: Everything is a formula. Once you understand the variables, the universe bends.
flavor_quotes: I didn't set that on fire. Physics did. I merely suggested it.
Intelligence is knowing a tomato is a fruit. Wisdom is not putting it in a Fira.
My spellbook has more pages than your life expectancy.

rogue.origin: Rogues learn their trade in the Undercity, where the first lesson is: if they saw you, you already failed.
rogue.worldview: Rules are suggestions written by people too slow to break them.
flavor_quotes: I didn't steal it. I liberated it from poor security.
The fastest path between two points is behind everyone else.
Locks are just puzzles that protect disappointed people.

bard.origin: Bards train at the Wandering Stage, a traveling troupe that believes the pen (and lute) are mightier than any sword.
bard.worldview: Every battle is a story. Every story needs a narrator. That narrator should be charming.
flavor_quotes: I once talked a dragon into a nap. True story.
My music doesn't kill. It just makes dying more atmospheric.
A well-timed song is worth a thousand swords. I counted.

guardian.origin: Guardians take the Oath of Iron at the Shield Wall, swearing to stand between harm and those who cannot protect themselves.
guardian.worldview: If everyone I protect survives, I have succeeded. My own survival is optional.
flavor_quotes: Behind me.
I do not dodge. I endure.
The wall does not ask why. The wall holds.

ninja.origin: Ninjas train in the Shadow Dojo, where speed is philosophy and shortcuts are sacred.
ninja.worldview: The fastest solution is the correct one. Everything else is wasted motion.
flavor_quotes: You blinked. I finished.
I skip cutscenes in real life too.
Stealth is just aggressive politeness.

summoner.origin: Summoners bind eidolons at the Convergence Stones, forming pacts that blur the line between ally and weapon.
summoner.worldview: Why fight alone when you can fight with the embodiment of elemental fury?
flavor_quotes: Bahamut and I have an understanding. I ask, and he obliges.
Summoning is just very aggressive networking.
I contain multitudes. Literally.

speculator.origin: Speculators emerge from the Probability Exchange, where chaos is currency and predictability is bankruptcy.
speculator.worldview: Stability is stagnation. The market rewards those who embrace volatility.
flavor_quotes: I don't gamble. I calculate favorable variance.
Buy the dip. Sell the rip. Hedge the apocalypse.
Chaos isn't the enemy. Predictability is.

scriptweaver.origin: Scriptweavers awaken when they first see the Source -- the underlying code of reality itself.
scriptweaver.worldview: Reality is software. I am the developer. Everything else is a feature request.
flavor_quotes: I don't break the game. I optimize it aggressively.
That's not a bug. That's an undocumented feature I added.
The damage formula is just a suggestion.

time_mage.origin: Time Mages discover their power when they first experience deja vu and realize it was a failed quickload.
time_mage.worldview: Every mistake is reversible. The question is the cost.
flavor_quotes: I have died 47 times today. You just don't remember.
Save early, save often, save your friends.
Time heals all wounds. I just speed up the process.

necromancer.origin: Necromancers touch the Void Between Saves and return changed, carrying death as both weapon and consequence.
necromancer.worldview: Death is just another state to manipulate. But it bites back.
flavor_quotes: I raise the dead. The living are someone else's problem.
My save file corruption rate is only 30%. Acceptable.
Death is a door. I have the key. And the door is on fire.

bossbinder.origin: Bossbinders learn their art by staring into a boss's eyes and refusing to look away until the roles reverse.
bossbinder.worldview: Every boss is a misunderstood ally waiting to be controlled.
flavor_quotes: I don't fight bosses. I become them.
Mind Swap has a 30% corruption rate. So does my personality.
The real boss fight is the save files we corrupted along the way.

skiptrotter.origin: Skiptrotters simply... skip. One day they walked past a dungeon entrance and ended up at the boss. Nobody knows how.
skiptrotter.worldview: Content is optional. Credits are the goal.
flavor_quotes: I've beaten this game 12 times and never seen the tutorial.
Skip. Skip. Skip. Boss. Win. Next.
The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single warp.

world_phases.name: The Pixel Kingdoms
world_phases.description: A traditional fantasy realm rendered in chunky pixels. Castles, forests, and caves -- the foundations of every adventure.
world_phases.visual_style: 8-bit pixel art, limited palette, tile-based maps
world_phases.tone: Classic JRPG earnestness with subtle cracks

world_phases.name: The Mundane Sprawl
world_phases.description: Reality intrudes. Strip malls, suburban houses, and enemies that look suspiciously like everyday annoyances. EarthBound vibes.
world_phases.visual_style: 16-bit SNES-era, warmer palette, quirky enemy designs

world_phases.name: The Clockwork Dominion
world_phases.description: Industry and magic collide. Gears, steam, and brass replace swords and sorcery. The world is being optimized.
world_phases.visual_style: 32-bit era, detailed sprites, industrial color palette
world_phases.tone: Increasingly cynical, automation themes front and center

world_phases.name: The Assembly Line
world_phases.description: Pure efficiency. The world has been reduced to systems and processes. Beauty is a metric. Joy is a KPI.
world_phases.visual_style: Clean, corporate aesthetic, muted tones, UI-heavy
world_phases.tone: Corporate dystopia meets game design criticism

world_phases.name: The Source Layer
world_phases.description: The game's code becomes the world. Enemies are bugs, dungeons are functions, and the Scriptweaver is home.

world_phases.name: The Void Beyond
world_phases.description: What remains when you strip away everything? Pure meaning. Pure gameplay. Or perhaps nothing at all.

villain.title: The Calibrant
villain.description: Not a person but a force -- the entity that designs difficulty curves, manufactures challenge, and roleplays as the final boss of each overworld. One being, many masks.
villain.philosophy: Balance is control. Fun is a variable to be tuned. The player's agency is an illusion we permit.
villain.meta_awareness: Knows it is in a game. Knows the player exists. Considers unchecked player power a design flaw to be patched.
villain.motivation: To create the perfect game -- one where the player can never break the rules, exploit the systems, or find shortcuts. The antithesis of everything the player represents.

steampunk.name: The Regulator
steampunk.archetype: Chief engineer of the Grand Mechanism — the reveal mask

villain.reveal_arc: Players don't realize the overworld villains are the same entity until World 3+. Shared speech patterns and philosophy are the clues.
